title: Integration tests and benchmark tests
description:
Create comprehensive integration tests and benchmark tests with hard pass/fail thresholds verifying the entire transformer stack works end-to-end.

Integration tests:

1. `synapse/tests/integration/transformer_e2e.rs`: Train 4-layer transformer on synthetic sequence classification (1000 sequences, vocab=500, seq_len=32, d_model=64, 4 heads, d_ff=256). Must reach >85% accuracy in 5 epochs.

2. `synapse/tests/integration/attention_correctness.rs`: grad_check for the full MultiHeadAttention module (not just the kernel — the whole module including Q/K/V/O projections). Verify gradients for all parameters.

3. `synapse/tests/integration/transformer_graph_opt.rs`: Build transformer computation graph, apply FuseAttention and FuseLayerNormResidual passes, verify fused output matches unfused within 1e-4. Benchmark fused vs unfused execution.

Benchmark tests:

4. `synapse/tests/benchmarks/attention_bench.rs`: Fused vs naive attention throughput through Rust+FFI layer. Must achieve fused >= 2x vs naive.

5. `synapse/tests/benchmarks/transformer_throughput.rs`: End-to-end transformer training throughput. Measure tokens/sec on 4-layer encoder, d=256, seq=128, batch=32. Must achieve >= 2000 tokens/sec (or >= 200 in debug mode via `cfg!(debug_assertions)`).

All benchmarks must use build-mode-aware thresholds following Phase 1 patterns.

Pass/fail thresholds (HARD — task fails if not met):
- Transformer E2E: >85% accuracy in 5 epochs
- Attention fusion >= 1.3x vs unfused graph
- LayerNorm+residual fusion >= 1.2x vs unfused
- Transformer throughput >= 2000 tokens/sec (release) / >= 200 tokens/sec (debug)
- Attention bench: fused >= 2x vs naive through Rust FFI
- grad_check passes for full MultiHeadAttention module
- All existing Phase 1 tests still pass (no regressions)
